assumption_statement: Milestone year 2025 is anchored to the latest available Australian baseline evidence, even when that evidence is reported for 2023–24 energy data, 2024 calendar-year electricity generation, or 2022–23 inventory data.
rationale: Australian official datasets are staggered in time. Using the latest official dataset family per parameter is preferable to forcing everything to one older year.
proposed_validation_route: Revisit when 2024–25 energy flows and newer inventory tables are released.

assumption_statement: output_cost_per_unit is defined as a real-2024 non-commodity conversion/supply cost, excluding explicitly modelled commodity purchases (electricity, gas, coal, fuels, hydrogen, biomass, scrap, iron ore, sequestration service) and excluding carbon costs unless explicitly noted.
rationale: This avoids double counting when commodity prices are endogenised or configuration-driven in the reduced-form model.
proposed_validation_route: Cross-check with implementation team before integrating into the reduced-form model.

assumption_statement: Direct electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use sector coefficients; electricity-related emissions are represented only in electricity-supply states.
rationale: Prevents scope-2 electricity emissions from being double counted with grid emissions intensity.
affected_sectors_parameters: Buildings, transport, industrial heat, steel, cement, agriculture, removals
proposed_validation_route: Test aggregate direct emissions versus inventory scope-1 sectors; optionally produce a separate scope-2 reporting view later.

assumption_statement: Building service outputs are represented as baseline-normalised service-equivalent GJ rather than detailed physical end-use outputs.
rationale: National reduced-form modelling needs a compact service metric with an upgrade path to explicit end uses later.
proposed_validation_route: In Phase 2, split space conditioning, water heating and plug loads and re-map service volumes.

assumption_statement: The 2025 residential and commercial incumbent states are normalised so that one unit of service corresponds approximately to one unit of 2025 final energy input under incumbent conditions.
rationale: This makes baseline calibration transparent using official sector final-energy totals.
proposed_validation_route: Compare with engineering bottom-up service models if later developed.

assumption_statement: Passenger-road incumbent energy intensity is calibrated to aggregate road-energy totals using BITRE passenger-km totals, so it reflects the stock-average fleet and road-use conditions rather than only a representative new car.
rationale: Using only new-vehicle test values would materially understate current aggregate road fuel demand.
proposed_validation_route: Check against vehicle-km, occupancy and fuel sales reconciliation in a transport stock model.

assumption_statement: Passenger-road service demand in baseline calibration is represented by national road passenger-km equal to passenger car plus bus motorised passenger travel.
rationale: This better matches the road-energy aggregate than using passenger cars alone.
proposed_validation_route: Replace with more detailed road-mode decomposition if required.

assumption_statement: Road-freight incumbent energy intensity is inferred from BITRE road tonne-km and Australian freight-emissions evidence indicating road freight is roughly one-third of transport emissions.
rationale: Australia lacks a single official direct road-freight final-energy-by-tonne-km table covering the full national task.
proposed_validation_route: Validate against detailed heavy-vehicle fuel-sales or freight stock datasets if available.

assumption_statement: Indicative direct combustion factors are taken from Australian NGA factors where available; a representative 0.09 tCO2e/GJ coal factor is used where the exact process-specific Australian factor was not directly sourced.
rationale: A reduced-form library needs consistent direct combustion factors across sectors even when sector-specific coal factors vary.
proposed_validation_route: Replace with subsector-specific coal factors in Phase 2.

assumption_statement: Biomass combustion is treated as zero direct CO2e in the energy_emissions field, with sustainability and upstream effects left outside Phase 1.
rationale: This matches common territorial inventory treatment for biogenic combustion but is incomplete for life-cycle assessment.
proposed_validation_route: Add sustainability constraints or upstream biomass emissions in Phase 2.

assumption_statement: Electricity supply is represented by a national frontier rather than separate state or NEM/non-NEM state sets in Phase 1.
rationale: A national reduced-form model can function with a single frontier, while regional detail is deferred for faster MVP delivery.
proposed_validation_route: Add NEM/non-NEM or state-region variants in Phase 2 if model behavior is materially sensitive.

assumption_statement: Electricity-state direct emissions trajectories are calibrated to recent NEM/emissions-projection benchmarks rather than derived solely from fuel coefficients.
rationale: System-average emissions intensity depends on dispatch, curtailment, storage and non-fossil resources, not just simple fuel heat rates.
proposed_validation_route: Compare against explicit regional dispatch models or ISP trace outputs later.

assumption_statement: Generic industrial heat is represented in three temperature bands (low, medium, high) with representative technology families, rather than subsector-specific processes.
rationale: This preserves cross-sector fuel-switch behavior while keeping Phase 1 tractable.
proposed_validation_route: Prioritise process-specific splits for alumina, food, chemicals and minerals in Phase 2.

assumption_statement: Steel route states represent Australian-feasible archetypes rather than a calibrated facility-by-facility plant model.
rationale: Facility-level Australian steel process data are too sparse for a rigorous full process-chain build in Phase 1.
proposed_validation_route: Replace with explicit BF-BOF, EAF, DRI shaft furnace and capture process chains in Phase 2.

assumption_statement: Hydrogen-DRI steel state aggregates both H2-DRI-EAF and H2-DRI with electric smelting/BOF-like finishing into one reduced-form near-zero state family.
rationale: These routes are distinct but serve a similar Phase 1 modelling role as primary near-zero steel pathways.
proposed_validation_route: Split into distinct route families in Phase 2.

assumption_statement: Cement is represented on a cement-equivalent output basis rather than clinker-only output so that clinker factor reduction and electricity intensity can be represented together.
rationale: Australia-specific industry data are readily available on both clinker/cement ratio and electricity per tonne cement.
proposed_validation_route: Add explicit clinker, SCM and cement milling process chain later.

assumption_statement: Cement process-emissions coefficients are derived from clinker-factor trajectories multiplied by clinker process CO2 factors from the Australian CIF pathway.
rationale: This is the most transparent Australia-specific way to convert pathway evidence into per-tonne cement-equivalent process emissions.
proposed_validation_route: Cross-check against plant-level disclosures if they become available.

assumption_statement: Agriculture is represented as two coarse output bundles: livestock and cropping/horticulture. Coefficients are allocated using official output values plus explicit judgement on emissions and energy shares.
rationale: A process-rich agricultural build is out of scope for Phase 1, but agriculture is too important to omit entirely.
proposed_validation_route: Replace with livestock category and fertiliser/soil-management detail in Phase 2.

assumption_statement: Agriculture output_cost_per_unit is treated as a placeholder incremental non-energy supply/abatement cost rather than a full production cost.
rationale: Robust full agricultural production-cost functions were not assembled in Phase 1.
proposed_validation_route: Develop explicit agricultural abatement supply curves in Phase 2.

assumption_statement: Land-sector sequestration states are exploratory supply states rather than a full land-use competition model.
rationale: The current evidence base is good enough to frame removals potential and constraints, but not to optimise land use rigorously.
proposed_validation_route: Integrate explicit land-use and carbon-forestry competition later.

assumption_statement: DACCS is included only as an exploratory engineered-removal state with highly uncertain cost and activity bounds.
rationale: Australian evidence is sparse and costs remain very uncertain, but it is useful to preserve an explicit placeholder for configuration testing.
proposed_validation_route: Do not activate in core MVP runs until policy treatment and cost assumptions are reviewed.

assumption_statement: Feasibility bounds (max_share and max_activity) are interpreted as indicative upper bounds or availability envelopes, not normative targets or endogenous diffusion curves.
rationale: The evidence base rarely supports precise uptake trajectories for every state family.
proposed_validation_route: Use sensitivity analysis and replace with explicit stock-rollover constraints later.

assumption_statement: Where observed baseline values are strong but prospective cost trajectories are sparse, real-2024 cost paths are smoothed across milestone years rather than fitted to a single source table.
rationale: This reduces false precision and keeps the library interpolation-ready.
proposed_validation_route: Refresh cost trajectories when better sector studies become available.

assumption_statement: The library is designed for reduced-form national modelling, not facility-level dispatch, regional network modelling, or a complete material-balance representation.
rationale: This keeps scope aligned with the Phase 1 MVP objective.
proposed_validation_route: Phase 2 should add process-chain depth where the MVP proves most sensitive.
